info.name: SnakeAI Backend API
info.description:
Postman collection for testing all SnakeAI backend REST controllers.

## Base URL
`http://localhost:8080`

## Endpoints
- **REST** — `/api/models` — CRUD operations on trained AI models
- **WebSocket** — `/ws/ai/train` — Real-time training session (use Postman WebSocket Request)
- **WebSocket** — `/ws/ai/play`  — Real-time play session (use Postman WebSocket Request)

## How to use
1. Start the backend: `docker compose -f docker-compose-backend-only.yml up --build`
2. Wait until you see 'Started Main' in the logs
3. Run the requests in order (folder: Models API)

variable.description: Model name used for get/delete tests. Set this after you have a trained model in DB.

item.name: Models API
item.description: REST endpoints for managing trained AI models — mapped to ModelRestController at /api/models

exec: pm.test('Response is a JSON array', function () {
pm.test('Each model has required fields', function () {
// Auto-save the first model name for subsequent tests
console.log('No models found - DB is empty. Train a model first via WebSocket /ws/ai/train');

request.description:
Returns a list of all trained AI models stored in the database.

**Controller**: `ModelRestController.listModels()`
**Response**: `List<TrainedAiModel>` — each entry has `name`, `episodesRun`, `efficiency`, `topScore`

**Expected response when DB is empty**: `[]`
**Expected response with data**:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Agent_20240101_120000",
    "episodesRun": 1000,
    "efficiency": 7.35,
    "topScore": 22
  }
]
```

exec: pm.test('Response has model entity fields', function () {
pm.test('Model name matches path variable', function () {
// This test requires a model to exist in the DB.
// Run '1. List All Models' first to auto-populate modelName,
// OR set the 'modelName' collection variable manually.

request.description:
Returns the full entity details for a specific trained model by its name.

**Controller**: `ModelRestController.getModel(name)`
**Path Variable**: `name` — the model's unique name (e.g., `Agent_20240101_120000`)
**Response**: `TrainedModelEntity` — includes `name`, `episodesRun`, `efficiency`, `topScore`, `filePath`, `createdAt`, `historyJson`

**Error case** — model not found:
```json
{
  "message": "Model TestAgent not found",
  "code": "RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND",
  "status": 404,
  "error": "Not Found",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "path": "/api/models/TestAgent"
}
```

exec: pm.test('Error response has required fields', function () {
// Verifies that GlobalExceptionHandler.handleNotFound() returned a proper ErrorResponse

request.description:
Intentionally requests a model that does not exist to verify the `GlobalExceptionHandler` correctly returns a 404 `ErrorResponse`.

**Tests**: Verifies `GlobalExceptionHandler.handleNotFound()` is wired correctly.

exec: // DELETE returns ResponseEntity<Void> — body should be empty
console.log('Run "1. List All Models" again to confirm deletion.');
// WARNING: This PERMANENTLY deletes the model from DB and filesystem.
// Make sure modelName is set to a model you want to delete.
// Run '1. List All Models' first to populate modelName automatically.

request.description:
Deletes a trained model from the database AND removes its `.zip` file from disk.

**Controller**: `ModelRestController.deleteModel(name)`
**Path Variable**: `name` — the model's unique name
**Response**: `204 No Content` / `200 OK` with empty body

⚠️ **Destructive operation** — the model file is permanently deleted from `/app/models/`.

exec: pm.test('Error response has correct structure', function () {

request.description: Attempts to delete a model that does not exist to verify the `GlobalExceptionHandler` correctly returns a 404.

item.name: WebSocket — AI Training
item.description:
WebSocket endpoint at ws://localhost:8080/ws/ai/train

Use Postman's 'New > WebSocket Request' for these.
Connect to the URL and send the JSON messages below.

These are documented here as HTTP requests for reference — copy the message body and paste it into the Postman WebSocket message box.

request.description:
## WebSocket Training Session

⚠️ **This is a WebSocket endpoint** — use Postman's **New > WebSocket Request** tab.

**URL**: `{{wsUrl}}/ws/ai/train`

---

### Message 1 — Start Training
```json
{
  "action": "START_TRAINING",
  "modelName": "{{modelName}}",
  "fieldSize": "MEDIUM",
  "hyperparameters": {
    "maxEpisodes": 100,
    "learningRate": 0.001,
    "batchSize": 64
  }
}
```

### Message 2 — Stop Training
```json
{
  "action": "STOP"
}
```

### Expected Server Response (every ~200ms while training)
```json
{
  "type": "TRAINING_METRICS",
  "metrics": {
    "episode": 42,
    "epsilon": 0.85,
    "loss": 0.0234,
    "averageReward": 1.5,
    "topScore": 10,
    "recentScore": 7,
    "stepsPlayed": 15000,
    "stepsPerSecond": 312.4,
    "elapsedTimeMs": 48000
  },
  "gameState": {
    "snake": [{"x": 5, "y": 5}, {"x": 4, "y": 5}],
    "food": {"x": 10, "y": 7},
    "score": 3,
    "status": "PLAYING",
    "direction": "RIGHT",
    "fieldSize": "MEDIUM"
  }
}
```

### FieldSize values
`SMALL` | `MEDIUM` | `LARGE` | `RANDOM`

item.name: WebSocket — AI Play
item.description:
WebSocket endpoint at ws://localhost:8080/ws/ai/play

Use Postman's 'New > WebSocket Request' for these.
Connect to the URL and send the JSON messages below.

request.description:
## WebSocket Play Session

⚠️ **This is a WebSocket endpoint** — use Postman's **New > WebSocket Request** tab.

**URL**: `{{wsUrl}}/ws/ai/play`

---

### Message 1 — Start Playing
```json
{
  "action": "START",
  "modelName": "{{modelName}}",
  "fieldSize": "MEDIUM",
  "tickRateMs": 120
}
```

### Message 2 — Pause
```json
{ "action": "PAUSE" }
```

### Message 3 — Resume
```json
{ "action": "RESUME" }
```

### Message 4 — Restart
```json
{ "action": "RESTART" }
```

### Message 5 — Stop
```json
{ "action": "STOP" }
```

### Expected Server Response (every `tickRateMs` ms)
```json
{
  "state": {
    "snake": [{"x": 5, "y": 5}, {"x": 4, "y": 5}],
    "food": {"x": 10, "y": 7},
    "score": 3,
    "status": "PLAYING",
    "direction": "RIGHT",
    "fieldSize": "MEDIUM"
  },
  "decisionMetrics": {
    "dangerStraight": 0.0,
    "dangerLeft": 1.0,
    "dangerRight": 0.0,
    "foodNorth": 0.0,
    "foodEast": 1.0,
    "foodSouth": 0.0,
    "foodWest": 0.0,
    "qValues": [0.75, -0.3, 0.12],
    "selectedAction": "STRAIGHT",
    "isExploration": false,
    "epsilon": 0.01
  }
}
```

item.name: Health & Diagnostics
item.description: Basic connectivity checks to verify the backend is up before running functional tests

exec: // Actuator endpoint may not be enabled — accept 200 or 404
console.log('ℹ️ Actuator not enabled — this is fine. Backend responded on port 8080.');

request.description:
Checks if Spring Boot Actuator health endpoint is available.

If you get `404`, that's fine — it means Actuator is not included as a dependency. The backend is still running.

To enable it, add to `build.gradle.kts`:
```kotlin
implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-actuator")
```

exec: pm.test('Backend is reachable on port 8080', function () {
console.log('✅ Backend is UP and responding on http://localhost:8080');
console.log('⚠️ Backend responded with status:', pm.response.code);

request.description: Quick connectivity ping — just calls `GET /api/models` to confirm the backend container is up and responding. Run this first after `docker compose up`.
